title: Barringtonia coccinea
intro: Barringtonia coccinea, commonly known as the red barringtonia or fire plum, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which are useful in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Barringtonia coccinea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Barringtonia coccinea is classified within the family Lecythidaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the Brazil nut or soapberry family. It belongs to the order Lecythidales, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily tropical in distribution. The genus Barringtonia is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The species Barringtonia coccinea is native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands, including Fiji and Samoa. The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the family Lecythidaceae.
